scenarios.description: children() on new Element with no child nodes returns empty list (childNodeSize==0)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); no children appended
pseudocode: TEST TC01 children on empty element

scenarios.description: children() on Element with one TextNode returns empty list after one filter iteration
pseudocode: TEST TC02 children filters out non-elements

scenarios.description: children() on Element with one child Element returns list of size 1
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("ul"); Element li = new Element("li"); parent.appendChild(li);
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains exactly li (size 1, element == li)
pseudocode: TEST TC03 children returns single child element

scenarios.description: children() on Element with two Element children returns both (loop-N where N=2)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); Element a = new Element("a"); Element b = new Element("b"); parent.appendChild(a); parent.appendChild(b);
pseudocode: TEST TC04 children returns two child elements
Element a = new Element("a"); Element b = new Element("b");
assert result.size() == 2; assert result.get(0)==a; assert result.get(1)==b;

scenarios.description: children() on Element with mixed Node types returns only Element children (loop-N where N=3)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("p"); parent.appendText("one"); Element span = new Element("span"); parent.appendChild(span); parent.appendText("two");
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements contains only span (size 1, element == span)
pseudocode: TEST TC05 children filters mixed nodes

scenarios.description: children() sets cache on first invocation (shadowChildrenRef null then non-null)
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); // reflectively ensure shadowChildrenRef is null before
scenarios.expectedResult: after el.children(), private field shadowChildrenRef.get() != null

scenarios.description: children() reuses cached list when shadowChildrenRef non-null and childNodes unchanged
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("a")); el.children(); // cache set; reflectively ensure shadowChildrenRef non-null
scenarios.expectedResult: second el.children() returns list equal to first (same reference from cache)
pseudocode: TEST TC07 children reuses cache when unchanged
Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("a"));

scenarios.description: children() invalidates cache after appendChild triggers nodelistChanged
scenarios.precondition: Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("a")); el.children(); // cache populated
scenarios.expectedResult: after el.appendChild(b), reflectively shadowChildrenRef.get()==null before next children()
pseudocode: TEST TC08 children invalidates cache on mutation
Element el = new Element("div"); el.appendChild(new Element("a")); el.children();

scenarios.description: children() returned list modification does not affect underlying childNodes
scenarios.precondition: Element parent = new Element("div"); Element e1 = new Element("e1"); Element e2 = new Element("e2"); parent.appendChild(e1); parent.appendChild(e2);
scenarios.expectedResult: calling result.clear() does not change parent.childNodeSize() (still 2)
pseudocode: TEST TC09 modifying children list does not affect nodes
Element parent = new Element("div"); Element e1=new Element("e1"); Element e2=new Element("e2");

scenarios.description: children() on Element with ten child Elements returns all (loop-N where N=10)
scenarios.precondition: Element parent=new Element("div"); for i in 0..9: parent.appendChild(new Element("li"));
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Elements size == 10 and all entries are instances of Element
pseudocode: TEST TC10 children returns ten elements
Element parent = new Element("div"); for(int i=0;i<10;i++) parent.appendChild(new Element("li"));
assert result.size() == 10; assert result.stream().allMatch(el->el instanceof Element);
